title: Terraform Layout
scope_summary: Describes the directory structure, apply sequence, and operational boundaries for AWS, non-AWS, and Snowflake Terraform roots. Establishes what Terraform may and may not provision, and delegates workload actions to explicit operator scripts.
locked_decisions: AWS and non-AWS Terraform roots are infra-only: must not create runnable application jobs/services, schedules, workflow engines, SQL procedures/tasks, dashboard apps, access-control bindings, or runtime secret values
AWS Terraform no longer accepts warehouse image, workflow schedule, app command, Snowflake trust principal, IAM role, or EDGAR identity value inputs
non-AWS Terraform no longer accepts container image, schedule, app command, MDM API key, Neo4j password, or DSN value inputs
Runtime does not use a runner IAM user; uses service-assumed roles: sec_platform_runner_execution, sec_platform_runner_task, sec_platform_runner_step_functions
MDM migrations/pipelines must be run only from operator-owned jobs or local operator commands, not from Terraform
key_requirements: Apply bootstrap-state before accounts/<env> to create Terraform state bucket
Populate runtime secrets out-of-band, not via Terraform
Apply access roots (IAM/RBAC/Snowflake grants) separately from infra roots
State migration required for environments where IAM/RBAC/Snowflake grant resources exist in old provisioning states
Apply dev before prod; do not apply prod until plans show no replacement for buckets, databases, or Snowflake objects
key_constraints: Terraform CLI pinned to 1.14.7 for AWS roots
S3 backend state locking uses use_lockfile = true
Bronze and warehouse data use separate buckets
Snowflake Terraform uses separate state keys and is not part of the AWS/non-AWS infra-only guarantee
